commonIssues:
Applicants must provide pre-1950 documents to verify caste, which is often difficult to produce.

How to fix:

  • Obtain old school records or admission registers of parents/grandparents from the respective schools.
  • Request Patta (land deed) or Chitta/Adangal records from the Taluk office — these often mention community.
  • Submit a sworn affidavit from two community elders along with their own certificates.
  • Apply via TN e-Sevai Portal and mention difficulty in the remarks section.

Revenue officers often conduct investigations in the applicant's grandfather's ancestral village to confirm the family historically followed the customs of that community.

How to fix:

  • Gather Nativity Certificate and community certificate of father/grandfather before applying.
  • Provide temple records, church records, or mosque records from the ancestral village if available.
  • Submit old voter ID or ration card of grandparents showing the ancestral village address.
  • Request elders from the village to provide a signed testimony about your family's community status.

Issues with address proof, particularly when relying on e-Sevai centres, can lead to rejection. Corrections to ration cards are required, which is a slow process.

How to fix:

  • Apply for ration card corrections via TN e-Sevai before starting the caste certificate application.
  • Use Aadhaar card or Voter ID as alternate address proof if ration card has errors.
  • Obtain an electricity bill or property tax receipt as supplementary address proof.
  • Visit the Taluk Office directly if e-Sevai centres are causing issues.

Small errors in names, addresses, or community names in applications lead to rejection.

How to fix:

  • Double-check all details against original documents before submission.
  • Verify the exact community name spelling in Tamil and English from the BC/MBC Welfare Department.
  • If already submitted with errors, apply for correction at the Revenue Divisional Officer (RDO) office.
  • Carry all original documents to the Taluk office for verification.

Those moving from other states struggle to get certificates, as there is no direct transformation provision.

How to fix:

  • Obtain a caste certificate from your state of origin first.
  • Submit school records from the native state showing community name.
  • Provide TN residence proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID) along with origin state certificate to the Tahsildar.
  • Approach the District Collector's office for inter-state verification assistance.

Many applicants face significant delays, missing college admission or scholarship deadlines.

How to fix:

  • Track status on TN e-Sevai Portal using your application number.
  • If delayed beyond 15 working days, file a grievance at the CM Cell Grievance Portal.
  • Invoke the TN Right to Service Act — escalate to the Appellate Authority (RDO).
  • For academic urgency, request an interim acknowledgement from the Tahsildar office.

When an application is returned, it may not appear in the 'Returned Menu' immediately, making re-application difficult.

How to fix:

  • Wait 24-48 hours for the returned application to appear in the system.
  • Check the 'Returned' section on the e-Sevai portal regularly.
  • If still not visible, visit the Taluk office in person with your application receipt.
  • File a complaint at the CM Cell or call 1100.

Increased surveillance by vigilance cells and caste scrutiny committees causes long delays.

How to fix:

  • Ensure all documents are genuine and consistent across records.
  • Cooperate fully during the field inquiry — keep all originals ready.
  • If cleared by the committee, follow up at the Tahsildar office for timely issuance.
  • If you feel unfairly targeted, lodge a grievance at the CM Cell.

Many feel fear or hesitation when approaching officials, leading to reliance on intermediaries/agents.

How to fix:

  • Apply online through e-Sevai to minimize direct interaction with officials.
  • If facing harassment, report to the CM Cell or call 1100.
  • Contact the District Legal Services Authority (DLSA) for free legal help — NALSA Portal.
  • Avoid using unauthorized agents — apply directly or through authorized e-Sevai centres.

Applications are not processed due to issues with CAN (Citizen Account Number) linkage.

How to fix:

  • Verify your CAN is active at the nearest e-Sevai centre.
  • If CAN is not linked, register for a new CAN at TN e-Sevai using Aadhaar.
  • Visit the Taluk office with your Aadhaar and application receipt for manual status correction.
  • Call the e-Sevai Helpline 044-2220 0500 for technical support.
